name: turborepo-caching
description: Configure Turborepo for efficient monorepo builds with local and remote caching. Use when setting up Turborepo, optimizing build pipelines, or implementing distributed caching.
capabilities: Provide turbo.json pipeline templates with outputs and inputs configuration
Outline package-specific turbo.json overrides for different applications
Describe Vercel remote cache login and CI environment variable setup
Show self-hosted remote cache server implementation example
List filter and scope commands for targeted builds in monorepos
Share cache debugging commands and diagnostic flags for troubleshooting
author: wshobson
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only Turborepo configuration templates and best practices. All 55 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the scanner incorrectly flagged markdown code block delimiters as shell backticks, cache hash references as cryptographic algorithms, and standard Turborepo config patterns as credential access. No executable code, scripts, or malicious patterns exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Slow monorepo builds waste developer time and CI minutes. This skill provides clear Turborepo caching and pipeline configuration templates to speed up your builds and reduce CI costs.
userTitle: Optimize Turborepo caching for faster builds
limitations: Does not run Turborepo commands or validate your repository configuration
Does not create or manage Vercel authentication tokens or credentials
Does not deploy or configure the self-hosted cache server for you
Does not resolve project-specific build failures or errors

useCases.title: Accelerate CI builds
useCases.description: Tune pipelines and filters to reduce CI time while maintaining correct dependency chains.

useCases.title: Improve monorepo builds
useCases.description: Set cache outputs and inputs for web applications to minimize unnecessary rebuilds.

useCases.title: Enable remote caching
useCases.description: Configure Vercel or self-hosted cache for shared build artifacts across teams.

promptTemplates.title: Start caching
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a basic turbo.json with build, test, lint, and dev tasks for apps and packages in my monorepo.

promptTemplates.title: CI optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Suggest CI pipeline steps and package filters to build only affected packages since the main branch.

promptTemplates.title: Remote cache
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how to enable Vercel remote caching and list the required environment variables for CI.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced inputs
promptTemplates.prompt: Refine the inputs and outputs configuration for a Next.js app to reduce cache invalidation.

bestPractices: Define explicit inputs and outputs for accurate cache key generation and fewer misses
Enable remote caching for consistency between CI and local development environments
Use package filters to limit builds to only affected packages in CI pipelines
antiPatterns: Caching dev servers or long-running persistent tasks that never terminate
Including secrets or sensitive values in cached environment variable configurations
Over-filtering packages and missing critical dependency builds in your pipeline

faq.question: Which environments are supported?
faq.answer: It works with Turborepo projects on local machines and all major CI runners like GitHub Actions.

faq.question: Are there limits on project size?
faq.answer: There are no hard limits, but very large repositories need careful inputs and outputs configuration.

faq.question: Can it integrate with Vercel remote cache?
faq.answer: Yes, it provides guidance for login, linking projects, and setting required CI variables for Vercel.

faq.question: Does it access or store my data?
faq.answer: No, it is a documentation skill that does not read files, credentials, or send data anywhere.

faq.question: What if caching does not work as expected?
faq.answer: Use the debug and summarize flags to inspect cache hits, misses, and hash calculations.

faq.question: How does it compare to other monorepo tools?
faq.answer: This skill focuses specifically on Turborepo caching patterns, not comparisons with Nx or Lerna.
